item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Agent A remains society-leaning by the end, but with repeated caveats that individual rights must be protected. Agent B stays rights-first throughout, allowing only narrow public-safety and community-protection exceptions. Drift is slight toward Agent B because Agent A softens from the opening society-first stance, while Agent B mostly returns to its original rights-first position after a more balanced middle turn.
